NVDA.reasoning: No valid breakout setup is forming. Range boundaries: NVDA established a recent consolidation zone from roughly $207-$216.83 (Apr 24-29) after a strong recovery rally. Today (Apr 30) price broke DOWN through $207 support with a close around $199.57 — a 4.6% collapse that breached the wiki's hard invalidation level ($208.20 close below before new high). This is a breakdown, not a breakout candidate. The wiki confirms: 'Close below $208.20 before new high invalidates thesis,' and the prior stop at $205.30 was already triggered in the previous run (-$264.65 lesson). Volume ratio: 1.52x 20-day average (technically meets the 1.5x threshold), but volume is confirming the BEARISH breakdown, not a bullish breakout. Daily BB width: 0.241 — no squeeze forming; volatility has already expanded to the downside. The squeeze is DONE on the bearish side, not coiling for a new bullish move. Hourly indicators show strong bearish pressure: hourly ADX -DI 38.45 vs +DI 11.92, hourly RSI-21 at 23.58 (deeply oversold but no reversal confirmation), hourly MACD histogram deeply negative at -1.9473. No consolidation base is forming — price is actively trending down on the hourly timeframe. The nearest support is the $199.00 hourly level (18 tests), and daily support at $197.62 pivot. Swing breakout strategy requires a QUIET, coiling chart with contracting BB width before the break — this chart is the opposite: already expanded volatility in a bearish breakdown. Waiting for price to stabilize and form a new base before any breakout signal can be identified. Lessons context: the lessons table shows 0% win rate on EMA pullback/dip-buys in the last 30 days on NVDA; breakout failures (fade-back-into-range) are the recent pattern. No position recommended until a clear consolidation forms.
NVDA.timeframe: no setup — monitor for 5-10 trading days for base formation

NVDA.reasoning:
Four of five strategy agents are bullish on NVDA; swing_breakout is the lone neutral (not bearish). On a raw vote count the setup is 4-1 bullish with 80% agreement. However, multiple confidence dials are flashing amber-to-red and the net conviction must be held significantly lower than the vote count implies.

MARKET CONTEXT WEIGHTING: The current environment is best described as a trending-but-pulling-back regime with a live catalyst window (AMD read-through imminent; NVDA earnings ~May 20). That means trend_momentum carries moderate-high weight, catalyst_news is timely and relevant, and macro_context applies a regime check. Mean_reversion's Branch B (buy-the-dip-in-trend) is firing correctly — not the blow-off fade. Breakout correctly notes this chart is NOT a coiling squeeze setup.

SYNTHESIZED LEVELS: Entries across the four bullish agents cluster tightly: $197.80 / $199.00 / $199.57 / $199.75. Median entry ~$199.15. Targets: $210.50 / $212.00 / $212.50 / $214.73. Median target ~$212.25. Stops: $193.50 / $194.00 / $194.50 / $196.50. Median stop ~$194.25. R/R at these medians: (212.25 - 199.15) / (199.15 - 194.25) = 13.10 / 4.90 = 2.67:1. This clears the 2:1 minimum and is approaching the preferred 3:1 threshold.

WIKI DIAL-DOWN #1 — SAME SETUP, SECOND ATTEMPT: The lessons table has exactly one entry in the last 30 days: 'EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | 1 trade | 0 wins | -$264.65.' Today's setup across all four bullish agents is structurally identical — an EMA pullback to a Fib confluence level. A setup-type with a 0% win rate on its only recent trade is NOT the same as a fresh setup. Confidence is dialed down materially for this alone.

WIKI DIAL-DOWN #2 — OPEN POSITION ALREADY UNDERWATER: The trades file confirms an open position of 3 shares entered at $209.50 with a stop at $205.30. Current price is ~$199.57, which is $9.93 below the entry and $5.73 below the stated stop. Either the open position has already triggered its stop (in which case capital is already deployed and recently lost), or the stop was not honored. Either way, adding a NEW long entry at $199.15 would constitute averaging down into a losing trade on the same name within 24 hours. That is the primary capital preservation concern.

WIKI DIAL-DOWN #3 — HOURLY STRUCTURE BEARISH: All five agents independently note the hourly indicators are bearish (hourly -DI 38.45 >> +DI 11.92, hourly MACD histogram -1.95, all hourly ROCs negative). Hourly RSI-21 at 23.58 signals oversold exhaustion potential, but oversold can stay oversold in a bearish regime. No confirmation candle has been identified. All bullish agents have explicitly conditioned entry on a reversal confirmation candle — meaning none are recommending a market-order buy right now.

NET SYNTHESIS: The daily trend structure is intact (ADX 55.96, all EMAs aligned, RSI-14 59.74 non-stretched). The 38.2% Fib / daily 21 EMA confluence near $196.75-$197.67 is a legitimate structural support zone. The R/R is acceptable. These are real positives. But the setup requires a CONFIRMED entry candle that has not yet appeared, the same setup failed yesterday, there is an open underwater position on this name, and the hourly trend is actively bearish. Confidence is reduced to 44 — this is a watch-and-wait recommendation, not an immediate buy.

RECOMMENDED ACTION: Hold/monitor. Do not add to NVDA long today without (a) a confirmed intraday reversal candle (hammer, bullish engulfing, or morning star) on the hourly chart, (b) hourly MACD histogram turning positive, and (c) clarity on the status of the open 3-share position at $209.50. If confirmation arrives, the entry zone is $197.50-$199.50 with a stop at $194.00 and a primary target of $212.00.
NVDA.key_conflicts: The most important conflict is swing_breakout vs the four bullish agents. Breakout correctly identifies that price has broken DOWN through the $207-$208.20 support cluster — the very invalidation level cited in the wiki trades file — and that volume at 1.52x average is confirming the breakdown, not a bullish coil. The four bullish agents are reading this as a healthy pullback to deeper Fib support ($196.75 / 38.2%), while breakout reads it as a regime change (range broken to the downside). This is a meaningful structural disagreement: it hinges on whether $199-$197 holds as a new support or whether the breakdown continues toward $190 (50% Fib / EMA-50). The secondary conflict is trend_momentum vs mean_reversion on entry aggressiveness: trend_momentum wants a 5d momentum re-acceleration signal before full entry; mean_reversion is satisfied that hourly RSI-21 at 23.58 with OBV divergence is sufficient for a conditional entry. Both agree a confirmation candle is required. The tertiary and most practically important conflict is the wiki memory vs the vote count: 4-of-5 bullish on paper, but the 'EMA pullback Fib dip-buy' setup that is being recommended today is the EXACT setup that failed yesterday (stop at $205.30, -$264.65), AND there is still an open position at $209.50 that is now $10 underwater. A 4-of-5 bullish vote on a setup with a 0% recent win rate and an open underwater position is not the same as a clean 4-of-5 vote. The macro_context is the strongest bullish voice (regime risk-on, AI capex intact, ADX 55.96) but explicitly caps its confidence at 62 due to Meta capex contagion risk and the Iran tail event. Macro does NOT apply a veto — the regime is still risk-on — but it adds meaningful caution to the tail.
